[[Category:Discrete Mathematics]]
[[sn:Chapter 5.1 relations]][[Category:Pages that existed before the Great November Outage]]
[[sn:Chapter 5.1 relations]][[sn:Chapter 5.1 relations]]Define relations as sets of ordered pairs. This makes functions


Use letter rho ρ

ρ = { (1, 2),(2,4),(3,7),(2,2)}


A relation is a subset of the [[cartesian product]] of two sets[[File:Cartesian_Product_qtl1.svg|thumb|Cartesian products]]x^2 + y^2 = 8

For the set of numbers{ -4 ... + 4} on each
{| class="wikitable"
|+
!
!-4
!-3
!-2
!-1
!0
!1
!2
!3
!4
|-
| -4
!-4
!-3
|
|
|
|
|
|
|
|-
| -3
!-4
!-3
|
|
|
|
|
|
|
|-
| -2
!-4
!-3
|'''(-2, -2)'''
|
|
|
|'''(-2, 2)'''
|
|
|-
| -1
!-4
!-3
|
|
|
|
|
|
|
|-
|0
!-4
!-3
|
|
|(0, 0)
|
|
|
|
|-
|1
!-4
!-3
|
|
|
|
|
|
|
|-
|2
!-4
!-3
|'''(2, -2)'''
|
|
|
|'''(2, 2)'''
|
|
|-
|3
!-4
!-3
|
|
|
|
|
|
|
|-
|4
!-4
!-3
|
|
|
|
|
|
|
|}
For all natural numbers or whatever there's an infinite number of pairs in the cartesian products

== Interesting relations ==
[[File:Properies of relations.png|thumb|Relation properties]]

# [[Reflexive relation]]
# [[Symmetric]]
# [[Transitive]]
# [[Antisymmetric]]
Adding elements of the cartesian product allows the achivement of all of these properties except antisymmettry. This means that the full cartesian product as 123 but not 4

== Relations examples ==
| is not symmettric: 1 | 2 but not 2 | 1

| is transitive: if a | b and b | c then a | c

| is antisymmetric: suppose x | y and y | x, 

* x and y are real numbers

== Closure of a relation ==
{{Main|Closure}}
Closure is another relation

== Poset ==
{{Main|Poset}}
[[Category:Pages]]
[[Category:Git synced pages]]
